titleStmt.title: SII 11.180: original edition by N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 180. (B.K. No. 268 of 1927-28.) KOP, BƒÄGALK≈å·π¨ TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT. ON A SLAB LYING BEHIND THE TEMPLE OF DURGAVVA.

summary.p: This mutilated record belongs to the period of VikramƒÅditya VI and mentions Pergga·∏çe Muddarasa, who is described as endowed with the office of the MahƒÅmƒÅtya (a great minister). It mentions rƒÅjadhƒÅnƒ´ Vikramapura. We know from inscriptions that Arasibƒ´·∏çi in the Hungund Taluk of the Bijapur District, was known as Vikramapura and as the secondary capital of the Western ChƒÅlukyas (See Epigraphia Indica, Vol. XV, p. 82). Since a portion of the inscription is chiselled away, it is not possible to make out the object of the record.
The inscription bears the date ChƒÅ·∏∑ukya-Vikrama year 10, Kr≈çdhana, ƒÄshƒÅ·∏çha, ≈õu. 1, Wednesday, Dakshi·πáƒÅyana-sa·πÖkrƒÅnti. Its English equivalent is A.D. 1085, June 25, Wednesday; ¬∑82.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded in TEI/EpiDoc according to the DHARMA Encoding Guide for Documentary Editions, version 2

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

pita mahƒÅmƒÅtyapadevƒ´virƒÅjamƒÅna mƒÅn≈çnna ta prabhuma·πÅtr≈çtsƒÅha≈õaktitraya
sa·πÅpannarappa ≈õrƒ´matpergga·∏çe muddarasar cƒÅ·∏∑ukyavikramavar·π£ada 10 neya kr≈çdhanasa·πÅva tsarada
